data.title: Vontobel Holding (VTX:VONN) shareholders have earned a 1.8% CAGR over the last five years
data.description:
Ideally, your overall portfolio should beat the market average. But the main game is to find enough winners to more than offset the losers So we wouldn't blame long term Vontobel Holding AG (VTX:VONN) shareholders for doubting their decision to hold, with the stock down 13% over a half decade.

So let's have a look and see if the longer term performance of the company has been in line with the underlying business' progress.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

There is no denying that markets are sometimes efficient, but prices do not always reflect underlying business performance. One way to examine how market sentiment has changed over time is to look at the interaction between a company's share price and its earnings per share (EPS).

While the share price declined over five years, Vontobel Holding actually managed to increase EPS by an average of 0.1% per year. So it doesn't seem like EPS is a great guide to understanding how the market is valuing the stock. Or possibly, the market was previously very optimistic, so the stock has disappointed, despite improving EPS.

Given EPS is up and the share price is down, it's clear the market is more concerned about the business than it was previously. Generally speaking, though, if the company can keep growing EPS then the share price will eventually follow.

You can see below how EPS has changed over time (discover the exact values by clicking on the image).

We know that Vontobel Holding has improved its bottom line lately, but is it going to grow revenue? Check if analysts think Vontobel Holding will grow revenue in the future.

Advertisement

What About Dividends?

It is important to consider the total shareholder return, as well as the share price return, for any given stock. The TSR is a return calculation that accounts for the value of cash dividends (assuming that any dividend received was reinvested) and the calculated value of any discounted capital raisings and spin-offs. So for companies that pay a generous dividend, the TSR is often a lot higher than the share price return. We note that for Vontobel Holding the TSR over the last 5 years was 9.5%, which is better than the share price return mentioned above. The dividends paid by the company have thusly boosted the total shareholder return.

A Different Perspective

Vontobel Holding provided a TSR of 3.9% over the last twelve months. Unfortunately this falls short of the market return. On the bright side, that's still a gain, and it's actually better than the average return of 1.8% over half a decade This could indicate that the company is winning over new investors, as it pursues its strategy. Keeping this in mind, a solid next step might be to take a look at Vontobel Holding's dividend track record. This free interactive graph is a great place to start.

For those who like to find winning investments this free list of undervalued companies with recent insider purchasing, could be just the ticket.

Please note, the market returns quoted in this article reflect the market weighted average returns of stocks that currently trade on Swiss exchanges.

data.title: Public companies among Discount Investment Corporation Ltd.'s (TLV:DISI) largest stockholders and were hit after last week's 10% price drop
data.description:
Advertisement

Key Insights

  • Discount Investment's significant public companies ownership suggests that the key decisions are influenced by shareholders from the larger public
  • The top 2 shareholders own 60% of the company
  • Insider ownership in Discount Investment is 23%

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

If you want to know who really controls Discount Investment Corporation Ltd. (TLV:DISI), then you'll have to look at the makeup of its share registry. We can see that public companies own the lion's share in the company with 30% ownership. In other words, the group stands to gain the most (or lose the most) from their investment into the company.

As a result, public companies as a group endured the highest losses last week after market cap fell by ₪142m.

Let's take a closer look to see what the different types of shareholders can tell us about Discount Investment.

View our latest analysis for Discount Investment

What Does The Lack Of Institutional Ownership Tell Us About Discount Investment?

Small companies that are not very actively traded often lack institutional investors, but it's less common to see large companies without them.

There could be various reasons why no institutions own shares in a company. Typically, small, newly listed companies don't attract much attention from fund managers, because it would not be possible for large fund managers to build a meaningful position in the company. Alternatively, there might be something about the company that has kept institutional investors away. Institutional investors may not find the historic growth of the business impressive, or there might be other factors at play. You can see the past revenue performance of Discount Investment, for yourself, below.

Discount Investment is not owned by hedge funds. The company's largest shareholder is Mega Or Holdings Ltd, with ownership of 30%. Meanwhile, the second and third largest shareholders, hold 30% and 11%, of the shares outstanding, respectively.

A more detailed study of the shareholder registry showed us that 2 of the top shareholders have a considerable amount of ownership in the company, via their 60% stake.

While studying institutional ownership for a company can add value to your research, it is also a good practice to research analyst recommendations to get a deeper understand of a stock's expected performance. Our information suggests that there isn't any analyst coverage of the stock, so it is probably little known.

Insider Ownership Of Discount Investment

While the precise definition of an insider can be subjective, almost everyone considers board members to be insiders. Company management run the business, but the CEO will answer to the board, even if he or she is a member of it.

Insider ownership is positive when it signals leadership are thinking like the true owners of the company. However, high insider ownership can also give immense power to a small group within the company. This can be negative in some circumstances.

Our information suggests that insiders maintain a significant holding in Discount Investment Corporation Ltd.. Insiders have a ₪289m stake in this ₪1.3b business. This may suggest that the founders still own a lot of shares. You can click here to see if they have been buying or selling.

General Public Ownership

The general public-- including retail investors -- own 17% stake in the company, and hence can't easily be ignored. This size of ownership, while considerable, may not be enough to change company policy if the decision is not in sync with other large shareholders.

Private Company Ownership

We can see that Private Companies own 30%, of the shares on issue. It's hard to draw any conclusions from this fact alone, so its worth looking into who owns those private companies. Sometimes insiders or other related parties have an interest in shares in a public company through a separate private company.

Public Company Ownership

Public companies currently own 30% of Discount Investment stock. We can't be certain but it is quite possible this is a strategic stake. The businesses may be similar, or work together.

Next Steps:

It's always worth thinking about the different groups who own shares in a company. But to understand Discount Investment better, we need to consider many other factors. Like risks, for instance. Every company has them, and we've spotted 3 warning signs for Discount Investment (of which 1 makes us a bit uncomfortable!) you should know about.

Of course, you might find a fantastic investment by looking elsewhere. So take a peek at this free list of interesting companies.

NB: Figures in this article are calculated using data from the last twelve months, which refer to the 12-month period ending on the last date of the month the financial statement is dated. This may not be consistent with full year annual report figures.

data.title: Tele2 AB (publ) (STO:TEL2 B) Full-Year Results Just Came Out: Here's What Analysts Are Forecasting For This Year
data.description:
As you might know, Tele2 AB (publ) (STO:TEL2 B) recently reported its yearly numbers. Tele2 reported in line with analyst predictions, delivering revenues of kr30b and statutory earnings per share of kr6.58, suggesting the business is executing well and in line with its plan. This is an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance in its report, look at what experts are forecasting for next year, and see if there has been any change to expectations for the business. Readers will be glad to know we've aggregated the latest statutory forecasts to see whether the analysts have changed their mind on Tele2 after the latest results.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Following last week's earnings report, Tele2's 16 analysts are forecasting 2026 revenues to be kr30.3b, approximately in line with the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to grow 19% to kr7.87. Yet prior to the latest earnings, the analysts had been anticipated revenues of kr30.3b and earnings per share (EPS) of kr7.83 in 2026. So it's pretty clear that, although the analysts have updated their estimates, there's been no major change in expectations for the business following the latest results.

See our latest analysis for Tele2

The analysts reconfirmed their price target of kr164, showing that the business is executing well and in line with expectations. Fixating on a single price target can be unwise though, since the consensus target is effectively the average of analyst price targets. As a result, some investors like to look at the range of estimates to see if there are any diverging opinions on the company's valuation. Currently, the most bullish analyst values Tele2 at kr200 per share, while the most bearish prices it at kr127. There are definitely some different views on the stock, but the range of estimates is not wide enough as to imply that the situation is unforecastable, in our view.

Of course, another way to look at these forecasts is to place them into context against the industry itself. We would highlight that Tele2's revenue growth is expected to slow, with the forecast 1.4% annualised growth rate until the end of 2026 being well below the historical 2.8% p.a. growth over the last five years. By way of comparison, the other companies in this industry with analyst coverage are forecast to grow their revenue at 4.8% per year. So it's pretty clear that, while revenue growth is expected to slow down, the wider industry is also expected to grow faster than Tele2.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that there's been no major change in sentiment, with the analysts reconfirming that the business is performing in line with their previous earnings per share estimates. On the plus side, there were no major changes to revenue estimates; although forecasts imply they will perform worse than the wider industry. The consensus price target held steady at kr164, with the latest estimates not enough to have an impact on their price targets.

Following on from that line of thought, we think that the long-term prospects of the business are much more relevant than next year's earnings. At Simply Wall St, we have a full range of analyst estimates for Tele2 going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here..

You still need to take note of risks, for example - Tele2 has 2 warning signs (and 1 which makes us a bit uncomfortable) we think you should know about.

data.title: What Does YOC AG's (ETR:YOC) Share Price Indicate?
data.description:
YOC AG (ETR:YOC), is not the largest company out there, but it received a lot of attention from a substantial price movement on the XTRA over the last few months, increasing to €12.30 at one point, and dropping to the lows of €7.00. Some share price movements can give investors a better opportunity to enter into the stock, and potentially buy at a lower price. A question to answer is whether YOC's current trading price of €7.00 reflective of the actual value of the small-cap? Or is it currently undervalued, providing us with the opportunity to buy? Let’s take a look at YOC’s outlook and value based on the most recent financial data to see if there are any catalysts for a price change.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What's The Opportunity In YOC?

Good news, investors! YOC is still a bargain right now according to our price multiple model, which compares the company's price-to-earnings ratio to the industry average. We’ve used the price-to-earnings ratio in this instance because there’s not enough visibility to forecast its cash flows. The stock’s ratio of 13.81x is currently well-below the industry average of 26.86x, meaning that it is trading at a cheaper price relative to its peers. What’s more interesting is that, YOC’s share price is quite volatile, which gives us more chances to buy since the share price could sink lower (or rise higher) in the future. This is based on its high beta, which is a good indicator for how much the stock moves relative to the rest of the market.

Check out our latest analysis for YOC

What does the future of YOC look like?

Investors looking for growth in their portfolio may want to consider the prospects of a company before buying its shares. Although value investors would argue that it’s the intrinsic value relative to the price that matter the most, a more compelling investment thesis would be high growth potential at a cheap price. YOC's earnings over the next few years are expected to double, indicating a very optimistic future ahead. This should lead to stronger cash flows, feeding into a higher share value.

What This Means For You

Are you a shareholder? Since YOC is currently trading below the industry PE ratio, it may be a great time to accumulate more of your holdings in the stock. With an optimistic profit outlook on the horizon, it seems like this growth has not yet been fully factored into the share price. However, there are also other factors such as capital structure to consider, which could explain the current price multiple.

Are you a potential investor? If you’ve been keeping an eye on YOC for a while, now might be the time to enter the stock. Its buoyant future profit outlook isn’t fully reflected in the current share price yet, which means it’s not too late to buy YOC. But before you make any investment decisions, consider other factors such as the track record of its management team, in order to make a well-informed assessment.

In light of this, if you'd like to do more analysis on the company, it's vital to be informed of the risks involved. Be aware that YOC is showing 4 warning signs in our investment analysis and 1 of those shouldn't be ignored...

If you are no longer interested in YOC, you can use our free platform to see our list of over 50 other stocks with a high growth potential.

data.title: Sandvik AB (publ) (STO:SAND) Just Reported And Analysts Have Been Lifting Their Price Targets
data.description:
Investors in Sandvik AB (publ) (STO:SAND) had a good week, as its shares rose 5.6% to close at kr352 following the release of its full-year results. It was a credible result overall, with revenues of kr121b and statutory earnings per share of kr11.70 both in line with analyst estimates, showing that Sandvik is executing in line with expectations. Following the result, the analysts have updated their earnings model, and it would be good to know whether they think there's been a strong change in the company's prospects, or if it's business as usual. We thought readers would find it interesting to see the analysts latest (statutory) post-earnings forecasts for next year.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Following the latest results, Sandvik's 19 analysts are now forecasting revenues of kr130.1b in 2026. This would be an okay 7.8% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to soar 21% to kr14.20. Yet prior to the latest earnings, the analysts had been anticipated revenues of kr129.9b and earnings per share (EPS) of kr14.05 in 2026. So it's pretty clear that, although the analysts have updated their estimates, there's been no major change in expectations for the business following the latest results.

Check out our latest analysis for Sandvik

The consensus price target rose 5.8% to kr326despite there being no meaningful change to earnings estimates. It could be that the analystsare reflecting the predictability of Sandvik's earnings by assigning a price premium. There's another way to think about price targets though, and that's to look at the range of price targets put forward by analysts, because a wide range of estimates could suggest a diverse view on possible outcomes for the business. There are some variant perceptions on Sandvik, with the most bullish analyst valuing it at kr380 and the most bearish at kr212 per share. As you can see, analysts are not all in agreement on the stock's future, but the range of estimates is still reasonably narrow, which could suggest that the outcome is not totally unpredictable.

Looking at the bigger picture now, one of the ways we can make sense of these forecasts is to see how they measure up against both past performance and industry growth estimates. We can infer from the latest estimates that forecasts expect a continuation of Sandvik'shistorical trends, as the 7.8% annualised revenue growth to the end of 2026 is roughly in line with the 8.8% annual growth over the past five years. Compare this with the broader industry, which analyst estimates (in aggregate) suggest will see revenues grow 5.2% annually. So although Sandvik is expected to maintain its revenue growth rate, it's definitely expected to grow faster than the wider industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that there's been no major change in sentiment, with the analysts reconfirming that the business is performing in line with their previous earnings per share estimates. Fortunately, they also reconfirmed their revenue numbers, suggesting that it's tracking in line with expectations. Additionally, our data suggests that revenue is expected to grow faster than the wider industry. There was also a nice increase in the price target, with the analysts clearly feeling that the intrinsic value of the business is improving.

Following on from that line of thought, we think that the long-term prospects of the business are much more relevant than next year's earnings. We have forecasts for Sandvik going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

And what about risks? Every company has them, and we've spotted 1 warning sign for Sandvik you should know about.

With shares of Waste Management trading around $222.24, investors are getting fresh information that goes beyond routine quarterly results. The combination of a higher dividend, a multibillion-dollar buyback, and a track record that includes a 54.1% return over 3 years and 111.4% over 5 years helps frame how NYSE:WM has rewarded long-term holders.

For you as an investor, the mix of capital returns and sustainability projects offers a clearer view of how management is deploying cash across income, buybacks, and infrastructure. data.title: Is Hexcel (HXL) Still Attractively Priced After Recent Aerospace Sector Attention?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Hexcel at around US$82.81 still offers value, it helps to first see how its recent share price behaviour lines up with what the fundamentals suggest.
  • The stock has had a mixed short term patch, with a 1.6% decline over the last 7 days, a 12.1% return over 30 days, a 7.7% return year to date, a 28.5% return over 1 year, a 22.1% return over 3 years, and an 80.6% return over 5 years.
  • Recent news around Hexcel has focused on the company as part of broader discussions on aerospace and advanced materials suppliers. This has kept investor attention on how it is positioned in that space and provides context for the share price moves as investors react to sector level themes and reassess what they are willing to pay for the stock.
  • On our valuation checks, Hexcel currently scores 2 out of 6 for being undervalued. We will unpack this using different valuation methods before finishing with a more rounded way to think about what the stock is really worth.

Hexcel scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Hexcel Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting them back to today using an appropriate rate. It is essentially asking what Hexcel’s future cash generation is worth in today’s dollars.

For Hexcel, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow stands at about $139.5 million. Analyst estimates and extrapolations point to projected free cash flow of around $496.0 million in 2035, with intermediate years ranging from $203.1 million in 2026 to $309.5 million in 2028, all in dollars and discounted back to present value.

Bringing these projected cash flows together, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $96.92 per share. Compared with the recent share price of around $82.81, the model implies Hexcel trades at roughly a 14.6% discount to this estimate, which indicates the shares may be undervalued on this measure.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Hexcel is undervalued by 14.6%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 868 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Hexcel.

Approach 2: Hexcel Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Hexcel, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand because it links what you pay directly to the earnings the business is generating today. It helps you see how many dollars the market is willing to pay for each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how quickly earnings are expected to grow and how risky those earnings are. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher multiple, while lower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower, more conservative P/E.

Hexcel currently trades on a P/E of 60.26x. That is close to the peer average of 60.44x, and above the broader Aerospace & Defense industry average of 41.63x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Hexcel is 31.94x. This Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of what a reasonable P/E could be for the company, given its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and specific risks.

Compared with simple peer or industry comparisons, the Fair Ratio aims to be more tailored to Hexcel’s own characteristics rather than relying on broad group averages. With the current P/E of 60.26x sitting well above the Fair Ratio of 31.94x, this indicates that the shares look expensive on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1417 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Hexcel Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, which are essentially your story about Hexcel, expressed through your own view of fair value and assumptions for future revenue, earnings and margins.

A Narrative links what you believe about the business to a simple financial forecast, and then to an estimated fair value that you can compare directly with Hexcel’s current share price to decide whether it looks attractive, fully valued or expensive to you.

On Simply Wall St, Narratives sit inside the Community page and are designed to be easy to use. You can adjust assumptions in a few clicks and see how your fair value view changes as new information such as news or earnings updates is reflected in the data.

For Hexcel, for example, one investor might build a Narrative that supports a relatively high fair value per share, while another might use more cautious assumptions and arrive at a much lower fair value. Seeing those side by side can help you decide which story you find more convincing.

Do you think there's more to the story for Hexcel? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Leidos Targets 2030 With ENTRUST Deal And OpenAI AI Push
data.description:
• Leidos Holdings (NYSE:LDOS) announced the acquisition of ENTRUST Solutions Group, expanding its reach in energy infrastructure services.
  • The company also entered a partnership with OpenAI to integrate generative and agentic AI tools into core government, health, defense, and infrastructure workflows.
  • Both moves are positioned as key steps in advancing Leidos’ NorthStar 2030 strategy and reshaping its business mix.

Leidos, a major contractor across defense, intelligence, civil, and health markets, is using the ENTRUST acquisition to add more exposure to energy infrastructure work. That places NYSE:LDOS in the middle of ongoing grid modernization, utility reliability, and critical infrastructure resilience efforts that many governments and utilities are prioritizing.

The OpenAI partnership points to a heavier use of AI in day to day missions for government and infrastructure clients, from workflow automation to decision support. For investors, these moves are essentially about where Leidos wants its revenue mix and capabilities to be by 2030, and how AI and energy infrastructure could influence that path.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Leidos Holdings by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Leidos Holdings.

Why Leidos Holdings could be great value

For investors, the ENTRUST deal and OpenAI alliance point to Leidos pushing deeper into two areas that many of its government and utility customers care about right now: grid reliability and AI-powered productivity. The roughly US$2.4b all-cash acquisition, partly funded with a US$1.4b bridge facility, increases Leidos’ exposure to regulated utility work. The OpenAI partnership is aimed at embedding generative and agentic AI in everyday workflows across defense, civil and health programs, where peers like Booz Allen Hamilton and Science Applications International are also active.

Advertisement

How this fits the Leidos Holdings narrative

The news lines up with the existing narrative that Leidos is leaning into AI integration and acquisitions to improve efficiency and support higher quality contracts. Management is also using deals like ENTRUST to shift the business mix toward more recurring, service-based work, which the narrative frames as helpful for earnings visibility and contract win rates across core federal and infrastructure customers.

Key risks and rewards investors are weighing

  • The ENTRUST acquisition increases Leidos’ presence in utility and infrastructure engineering, which could deepen relationships with large, long-cycle customers.
  • Wider AI use through OpenAI’s tools may support faster product delivery and lower indirect costs for both internal teams and client projects.
  • Funding the US$2.4b deal with new debt and liquidity adds to balance sheet commitments, and analysts have already flagged that Leidos carries a high level of debt.
  • Acquisition integration and AI execution risk remain, especially as peers like RTX and Booz Allen compete aggressively for similar government and infrastructure budgets.

What to watch next

From here, you may want to track regulatory approvals and closing timing for the ENTRUST deal, how quickly Leidos wins new utility or grid-modernization work, and any commentary on AI adoption from upcoming earnings calls and investor updates. If you want to see how other investors are thinking about this story, check community narratives on the Leidos Holdings page and compare this news with the longer term thesis you already have in mind.

data.title: How Investors May Respond To American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) Exiting Quiet Logistics To Refocus On Core Brands
data.description:
• American Eagle Outfitters recently moved to wind down its Quiet Logistics subsidiary, which it bought for about US$360.00 million a little over four years ago, exiting third‑party logistics and closing several fulfillment centers while retaining the Atlanta site for its own distribution needs.
  • This retreat from supply chain services underscores management’s renewed emphasis on extracting more value from American Eagle’s existing retail brands and store base rather than expanding into adjacent logistics businesses.
  • We’ll now explore how exiting Quiet Logistics and refocusing on core lifestyle brands could reshape American Eagle Outfitters’ investment narrative.

These 13 companies survived and thrived after COVID and have the right ingredients to survive Trump's tariffs. Discover why before your portfolio feels the trade war pinch.

Advertisement

What Is American Eagle Outfitters' Investment Narrative?

For American Eagle Outfitters, the core investment case still comes back to believing the company can squeeze more earnings out of a relatively mature revenue base, through disciplined inventory, tighter cost control and better productivity from its existing stores and brands. Recent guidance upgrades for 2025 support that near term earnings focus, but the decision to wind down Quiet Logistics slightly reshapes the story: instead of testing adjacent profit pools in logistics, management is doubling down on its lifestyle brands and its own distribution footprint. In the short term, the Quiet exit looks more operational than transformational, with limited obvious impact on key catalysts like comparable sales trends, margin execution and capital returns, although any related closure costs could weigh on reported results. The more immediate risk remains execution in a slow growth, highly competitive apparel market.

However, investors should be aware that execution missteps may matter more now that growth is modest. Despite retreating, American Eagle Outfitters' shares might still be trading 19% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Seven Simply Wall St Community fair value views span roughly US$10 to just under US$29 per share, reflecting very different expectations, while the Quiet Logistics exit keeps the spotlight squarely on how efficiently American Eagle can run and grow its core brands.

data.title: Is DICK'S Sporting Goods (DKS) Pricing Reflecting Long Term Gains Or Recent Pullback
data.description:
• If you are looking at DICK'S Sporting Goods and wondering whether the current share price offers solid value, this article will walk you through what the numbers actually say rather than the headlines.
  • The stock closed around US$202.00 recently, with returns of 2.0% over the last 30 days, a 1% gain year to date, a 13.9% decline over the last year, and a 60.2% and 210.4% gain over the last 3 and 5 years respectively.
  • Recent coverage around DICK'S Sporting Goods has focused on how investors are weighing those longer term gains against the more recent 1 year pullback, as sentiment around retailers has shifted over different time frames. This mix of short term caution and longer term strength is an important backdrop when you think about what a fair price for the stock might look like.
  • On our checklist driven valuation framework, DICK'S Sporting Goods scores 3 out of 6 for value, giving it a 3/6 valuation score. Next we will look at how traditional methods such as P/E, cash flow and asset based metrics line up with that, before finishing with a way of thinking about valuation that can help you make sense of all these different signals.

Find out why DICK'S Sporting Goods's -13.9% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: DICK'S Sporting Goods Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of the cash a business may generate in the future and discounts those cash flows back to today to arrive at an estimated value per share.

For DICK'S Sporting Goods, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $293.0 million. Analysts provide inputs out to 2027, with free cash flow for that year assumed at $522.8 million, and Simply Wall St extrapolates this further. By 2035, the projected free cash flow in the model is $468.4 million, with each year discounted back to today.

Putting all of these discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of about $69.74 per share. Compared with the recent share price around $202.00, the DCF output implies the stock is 189.7% overvalued on this set of assumptions.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests DICK'S Sporting Goods may be overvalued by 189.7%. Discover 868 undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for DICK'S Sporting Goods.

Approach 2: DICK'S Sporting Goods Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like DICK'S Sporting Goods, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand for how much investors are paying today for each dollar of current earnings, which is often easier to relate to than cash flow models.

What counts as a normal or fair P/E usually reflects two big things: how fast earnings are expected to grow and how risky those earnings are. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk tends to point to a lower one.

DICK'S Sporting Goods is currently trading on a P/E of about 17.8x. That sits below the Specialty Retail industry average of roughly 19.5x and well below the peer group average of about 35.4x. Simply Wall St also calculates a Fair Ratio for the stock at around 20.9x, which is the P/E it might typically trade on given its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics.

This Fair Ratio is usually more tailored than a simple comparison to peers or the sector because it folds in company specific factors instead of relying on broad group averages. With the current 17.8x P/E sitting under the 20.9x Fair Ratio, this lens points to the shares looking undervalued on earnings.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1417 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your DICK'S Sporting Goods Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Let us introduce you to Narratives, a simple tool on Simply Wall St's Community page. With Narratives, you connect your story about DICK'S Sporting Goods to the numbers by setting your own assumptions for future revenue, earnings, margins and fair value. You can then compare that fair value to the current price to help decide when you might buy or sell. The platform keeps updating your Narrative as new news or earnings arrive. As a result, one investor might build a Narrative that assumes a much higher fair value for the stock than another investor who uses more cautious assumptions and arrives at a much lower fair value, even though both are looking at the same company.

Do you think there's more to the story for DICK'S Sporting Goods? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Siemens’ Automation and Energy Storage Push Might Change The Case For Investing In Siemens (XTRA:SIE)
data.description:
• In late January 2026, Siemens was highlighted across several industry reports for advancing robotics for data centers, AI-driven construction analytics, innovative electric control panels, and modular battery energy storage systems.
  • These developments underscore how Siemens is embedding automation, AI, and grid-support technologies across infrastructure and energy markets, reinforcing its role as a key technology enabler.
  • We’ll now examine how Siemens’ push into modular battery energy storage and broader automation solutions shapes the company’s investment narrative.

This technology could replace computers: discover 22 stocks that are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

What Is Siemens' Investment Narrative?

For Siemens, the core belief you need to have as a shareholder is that industrial automation, software and electrification will keep tightening their grip on global infrastructure, and that Siemens can keep selling higher‑value systems into that shift. The latest news on robotics for data centers, AI construction analytics, intelligent control panels and modular battery storage fits neatly into that story, but is unlikely to change the near term financial picture on its own. In the short run, the more material angles look like continued execution in software‑rich automation and disciplined capital allocation, including the ongoing share buyback and steadily rising dividend. The flip side is that Siemens already trades on a premium earnings multiple, carries meaningful debt and has seen margins slip, so expectations are not low if growth in these newer areas takes time to show up.

However, investors should also weigh how Siemens’ high valuation and debt could amplify any slowdown. Siemens' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 14%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The Simply Wall St Community’s 11 fair value estimates span about €208 to nearly €299, reflecting very different expectations. data.title: RVRC Holding (OM:RVRC) Margin Drift To 15.5% Tests Growth Focus After Q2 Earnings
data.description:
RVRC Holding (OM:RVRC) has reported a busy Q2 2026, with revenue of SEK726 million and EPS of SEK1.30 setting the tone for its latest results. The company’s revenue increased from SEK684 million in Q2 2025 to SEK726 million in Q2 2026, while EPS over the same quarters moved from SEK1.12 to SEK1.30. Trailing twelve month EPS of SEK2.85 frames the current earnings power. For investors, a key consideration now is how the solid top line and EPS profile compare with a net profit margin that has eased over the past year.

See our full analysis for RVRC Holding.

With the latest numbers in hand, the next step is to consider how this earnings picture aligns with the prevailing growth focused narrative around RVRC, and where the data might challenge those expectations.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Margins Ease To 15.5% On Trailing Basis

  • RVRC earned SEK312 million in net income on SEK2,008 million of trailing twelve month revenue, which works out to a 15.5% net profit margin compared with 16.2% a year earlier.
  • For a generally bullish view that focuses on growth, a key point is that this 15.5% margin is slightly lower than the prior 16.2%. Investors weighing the growth story against profitability can see that earnings power is supported more by SEK2,008 million of trailing revenue than by margin expansion.
    • Supporters of the growth angle can point to trailing EPS of SEK2.85 alongside that 15.5% margin, showing that absolute profits of SEK312 million are still healthy even as margin has eased.
    • On the other hand, anyone worried about margin pressure can see a clear data point in the shift from 16.2% to 15.5%, which they may compare against the revenue trend when judging how repeatable the current earnings profile looks.

Q2 Net Income Of SEK140 Million Stands Out

  • In Q2 2026, RVRC reported SEK140 million of net income on SEK726 million of revenue, versus SEK125 million on SEK684 million in Q2 2025, so this single quarter contributed a sizeable share of the SEK312 million earned over the last twelve months.
  • For a generally bullish growth angle, this Q2 snapshot with SEK140 million of profit sits within a run of quarterly net income figures between SEK46 million and SEK140 million and EPS between SEK0.41 and SEK1.30. Investors who like the growth story can point to multiple profitable quarters while also seeing that performance does move around within the year.
    • The sequence of quarterly revenue figures from SEK350 million to SEK726 million, paired with positive net income in each quarter, supports the idea that RVRC is producing consistent profits rather than relying on a single period.
    • At the same time, the spread between the lower profit quarters, such as SEK46 million, and Q2's SEK140 million encourages investors to pay attention to seasonality and not assume that the strongest quarter reflects a full year run rate.
To see how this earnings pattern fits into the broader growth story, including risks and valuation, you can read the full narrative range for RVRC Holding. 📊 Read the full RVRC Holding Consensus Narrative.

Growth Metrics Contrast With Mixed Valuation Signals

  • Over the past five years, earnings grew about 9.4% per year and are forecast at roughly 11.6% per year, while revenue is forecast at about 9.9% per year. The current P/E of 23x sits between a peer average of 39x and a European Specialty Retail average of 17.3x, and the share price of SEK68.25 is about 19.5% below the cited DCF fair value of SEK84.82.
  • For investors who lean bullish, the combination of multi year earnings growth around 9.4% per year, forecast revenue growth of about 9.9% per year and a share price below the DCF fair value while the P/E remains above the broader industry average presents a mix of supportive growth data and valuation trade offs that they need to weigh carefully.
    • The P/E discount to the 39x peer average can be seen as aligning with the idea that the stock is not priced at the top of its group even after several years of earnings growth.
    • However, the premium to the 17.3x European Specialty Retail average and the recent margin move from 16.2% to 15.5% give investors concrete numbers to consider when deciding how much weight to place on the growth and DCF signals.

Next Steps

Don't just look at this quarter; the real story is in the long-term trend. We've done an in-depth analysis on RVRC Holding's growth and its valuation to see if today's price is a bargain. Add the company to your watchlist or portfolio now so you don't miss the next big move.

See What Else Is Out There

RVRC’s easing net margin from 16.2% to 15.5% and mixed P/E signals against peers highlight that the growth story comes with valuation trade offs.

If that mix of softer margins and an uncertain valuation trade off does not quite fit your comfort zone, use these 868 undervalued stocks based on cash flows today to focus on companies where pricing looks more clearly aligned with their financial profile.

data.title: Is New Found Gold’s (TSXV:NFG) EPCM Move Quietly Redefining Its Risk Profile And Capital Needs?
data.description:
• New Found Gold Corp. has moved its Queensway Gold Project in Newfoundland and Labrador further toward potential production, launching the 2026 drill program, resuming diamond drilling, and signing a Phase 1 engineering, procurement and construction management contract with WSP Canada for mill upgrades and site development.
  • Ongoing infill drilling at the K2 zone, preparation of an updated mineral resource estimate, and progress on environmental permitting together signal a transition from pure exploration toward early-stage project development and financing work.
  • We will now examine how the EPCM contract with WSP Canada and related permitting and drilling activity shape New Found Gold's investment narrative.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 110 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Is New Found Gold's Investment Narrative?

For someone considering New Found Gold, the core belief is that Queensway can progress from a promising discovery into a viable producing asset, despite the company’s lack of revenue and ongoing losses. The new EPCM contract with WSP Canada, the planned Pine Cove mill upgrades, and the active 2026 drill program all push the story further into early development, adding clearer short term catalysts around permitting milestones, the updated mineral resource estimate, and future technical reports. At the same time, this accelerates exposure to execution risk: a relatively new management and board must now manage complex engineering, permitting and future financing decisions, in a market that has recently marked the shares down after a strong 1 year run. Whether the recent pullback reflects opportunity or concern will depend on confidence in that transition.

Yet this pivot toward development introduces a risk many investors may not have fully considered.

Despite retreating, New Found Gold's shares might still be trading 28% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Three fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly CA$0.49 to CA$4.86 per share, underscoring how differently private investors view Queensway’s potential. Set that against management’s rapid move into EPCM, permitting and resource updates, and you can see why opinions on New Found Gold’s next phase and its impact on future performance are so far apart. Readers may want to weigh several of these perspectives before forming their own view.

data.title: EQT’s Growing Scale Reliability And Community Focus In Investor Spotlight
data.description:
• EQT recently became the sixth-largest oil and gas producer in the US, with a growing presence among Appalachian producers.
  • The company maintained stable output during severe winter storms that disrupted parts of the US energy sector.
  • Through the EQT Foundation, the company has recently supported K-12 STEM and arts education programs as part of its community focus.

For investors watching NYSE:EQT, these developments come with a share price of $57.73 and a reported multi year total return of 14.3% over the past year and 266.9% over five years. EQT's scale as a top six US producer, combined with its Appalachian focus, places it in a prominent spot among US natural gas companies.

The recent storm performance and philanthropic activity may influence how you think about EQT's risk profile and corporate priorities. As you follow future updates, you might consider how operational reliability and community engagement fit into your broader thesis on the stock and the energy sector overall.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for EQT by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on EQT.

Why EQT could be great value

EQT’s move into the sixth-largest slot among US oil and gas producers, combined with stable output through severe winter storms, signals to investors that the company is operating at true large cap scale with an emphasis on reliability. For a gas-focused producer competing with peers such as Chesapeake Energy and Coterra, the ability to keep volumes steady when infrastructure is under stress can matter for how the market views cash flow resilience and contract reliability.

Advertisement

EQT narrative, scale and social footprint

The grant activity through the EQT Foundation lines up with existing investor narratives that focus on long-term gas demand from power and AI data centers, because it shows the company trying to maintain a social licence to operate in the Appalachian communities where that growth would occur. For readers following the bullish and consensus EQT narratives, this mix of operational scale, energy transition positioning and visible community spending can be seen as part of the broader story around how EQT tries to differentiate itself from other natural gas names.

Risks and rewards that investors are weighing

  • Growing into a top-six US producer reinforces EQT’s relevance to larger buyers and counterparties, which can help when negotiating long-term contracts.
  • Operational resilience during the cold front supports the view that EQT’s integrated Appalachian footprint can keep gas flowing when conditions are difficult.
  • Larger scale and higher profile can draw more scrutiny to environmental practices and capital allocation, which may influence future project approvals and costs.
  • Philanthropic spending, while relatively small, still competes with other uses of cash such as debt reduction or shareholder returns if commodity conditions soften.

What to watch next

From here, you might watch how EQT talks about winter storm performance, community investment and its integrated gas projects on upcoming calls, and whether those points show up as a factor in analyst commentary compared with peers like EOG Resources and Devon Energy. If you want to see how this latest news fits into the broader long-term story investors are debating, take a look at community narratives on EQT and compare the assumptions that different analysts are using.

data.title: Aurubis AG's (ETR:NDA) Stock On An Uptrend: Could Fundamentals Be Driving The Momentum?
data.description:
Most readers would already be aware that Aurubis' (ETR:NDA) stock increased significantly by 42% over the past three months. As most would know, fundamentals are what usually guide market price movements over the long-term, so we decided to look at the company's key financial indicators today to determine if they have any role to play in the recent price movement. Specifically, we decided to study Aurubis' ROE in this article.

Return on equity or ROE is an important factor to be considered by a shareholder because it tells them how effectively their capital is being reinvested. In other words, it is a profitability ratio which measures the rate of return on the capital provided by the company's shareholders.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

How Do You Calculate Return On Equity?

Return on equity can be calculated by using the formula:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for Aurubis is:

11% = €539m ÷ €5.0b (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

The 'return' is the amount earned after tax over the last twelve months. That means that for every €1 worth of shareholders' equity, the company generated €0.11 in profit.

View our latest analysis for Aurubis

Why Is ROE Important For Earnings Growth?

Thus far, we have learned that ROE measures how efficiently a company is generating its profits. Depending on how much of these profits the company reinvests or "retains", and how effectively it does so, we are then able to assess a company’s earnings growth potential. Assuming everything else remains unchanged, the higher the ROE and profit retention, the higher the growth rate of a company compared to companies that don't necessarily bear these characteristics.

Aurubis' Earnings Growth And 11% ROE

To begin with, Aurubis seems to have a respectable ROE. On comparing with the average industry ROE of 8.4% the company's ROE looks pretty remarkable. As you might expect, the 2.7% net income decline reported by Aurubis is a bit of a surprise. We reckon that there could be some other factors at play here that are preventing the company's growth. Such as, the company pays out a huge portion of its earnings as dividends, or is faced with competitive pressures.

However, when we compared Aurubis' growth with the industry we found that while the company's earnings have been shrinking, the industry has seen an earnings growth of 2.3% in the same period. This is quite worrisome.

The basis for attaching value to a company is, to a great extent, tied to its earnings growth. The investor should try to establish if the expected growth or decline in earnings, whichever the case may be, is priced in. This then helps them determine if the stock is placed for a bright or bleak future. What is NDA worth today? The intrinsic value infographic in our free research report helps visualize whether NDA is currently mispriced by the market.

Is Aurubis Using Its Retained Earnings Effectively?

Aurubis' low three-year median payout ratio of 16% (or a retention ratio of 84%) over the last three years should mean that the company is retaining most of its earnings to fuel its growth but the company's earnings have actually shrunk. The low payout should mean that the company is retaining most of its earnings and consequently, should see some growth. So there could be some other explanations in that regard. For example, the company's business may be deteriorating.

Moreover, Aurubis has been paying dividends for at least ten years or more suggesting that management must have perceived that the shareholders prefer dividends over earnings growth. Looking at the current analyst consensus data, we can see that the company's future payout ratio is expected to rise to 26% over the next three years. Accordingly, the expected increase in the payout ratio explains the expected decline in the company's ROE to 7.6%, over the same period.

Summary

On the whole, we do feel that Aurubis has some positive attributes. However, given the high ROE and high profit retention, we would expect the company to be delivering strong earnings growth, but that isn't the case here. This suggests that there might be some external threat to the business, that's hampering its growth. Additionally, the latest industry analyst forecasts show that analysts expect the company's earnings to continue to shrink in the future. Are these analysts expectations based on the broad expectations for the industry, or on the company's fundamentals? Click here to be taken to our analyst's forecasts page for the company.

data.title: Should Coupang’s (CPNG) US$900 Million Data-Breach Penalty Risk Require Action From Investors?
data.description:
• In recent months, Coupang has been hit by multiple securities class action filings and regulatory probes after a data breach exposed tens of millions of customer accounts and allegedly went undisclosed for nearly six months.
  • The fallout now extends beyond cybersecurity, as South Korea’s Fair Trade Commission weighs very large penalties while US officials pressure Seoul over its treatment of American tech firms like Coupang.
  • Against this backdrop of mounting regulatory risk, we’ll examine how the potential US$900 million fine shapes Coupang’s investment narrative.

We've found 14 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

What Is Coupang's Investment Narrative?

To own Coupang today, you really have to believe that its core e‑commerce and logistics engine can keep compounding through volatility, while management absorbs a hit from the data breach without derailing the business. Before the scandal, the big catalysts were earnings growth expectations, continued profitability after a long path to the black, and capital returns via buybacks, all while the stock traded well below consensus fair value. The alleged six‑month, undisclosed breach and potential US$900 million KFTC fine now push legal and regulatory outcomes to the front of the story, at least in the short term, crowding out cleaner earnings and growth narratives. The recent share price resilience, despite sharp earnings downgrades, suggests the market is still weighing how permanent this damage will be.

However, one regulatory decision could sharply reshape Coupang’s near term profit profile and risk. Coupang's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 15%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Eight members of the Simply Wall St Community see Coupang’s fair value between about US$23.85 and US$40, yet the unresolved data breach penalties and class actions could materially influence how those projections play out. You can compare these community views with the shifting regulatory risk and earnings catalysts to test how your own expectations line up.

data.description:

Werner Enterprises stock at a glance

Werner Enterprises (WERN) has drawn investor interest recently, with the share price at $34.25 and total returns showing mixed signals across the past year, month and past 3 months.

See our latest analysis for Werner Enterprises.

The recent share price move to $34.25 comes after a strong 30-day share price return of 14.13% and a 90-day share price return of 30.73%. However, the 1-year total shareholder return decline of 3.26% and 3-year total shareholder return decline of 26.28% indicate that longer-term momentum has been weaker.

If Werner’s recent rebound has you reassessing the trucking space, it could be a good moment to compare it with other auto and freight names using auto manufacturers.

With Werner shares at $34.25 after strong short term returns but weaker 1 and 3 year results, the key question is simple: is the recent optimism still attractive value or already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 71.2% Overvalued

Werner Enterprises' most followed narrative pegs fair value at $20.00, well below the current $34.25 share price, which sets a cautious tone.

The rapid acceleration of automation, AI, and emerging logistics technology threatens to displace traditional trucking models, forcing Werner Enterprises to make increasingly large, ongoing capital investments just to remain competitive. This will likely drive significant increases in capital expenditures, erode return on invested capital, and compress net margins over time as technology driven players outpace Werner's current operational improvements.
